codeReview.summary: The feature adds substantial supplier and purchasing changes (approval endpoints, enum-backed statuses, encrypted bank fields, migration, and purchasing flow updates), and it successfully covers key return and GRN safeguards. However, it does not fully satisfy the declared PO lifecycle requirement in the feature definition and expected behavior because DRAFT and VOID transitions are not reachable via implemented purchasing APIs/service flows, while PO creation force-sets status to APPROVED.

issues.description: Feature description requires canonical PO lifecycle 'DRAFT -> APPROVED -> PARTIALLY_RECEIVED -> FULLY_RECEIVED -> INVOICED -> CLOSED -> VOID', but createPurchaseOrder persists new POs directly as APPROVED and there are no service/controller transitions that materialize DRAFT or VOID states in runtime flows.

Only create/list/get endpoints are exposed for PO operations; there are no workflow endpoints (e.g., approve/void/cancel transitions) to realize the full lifecycle asserted by the feature scope and expected behavior.

SupplierServiceTest coverage is minimal and only validates payable-account parent linking. It does not verify newly introduced supplier workflow/business fields (status transitions, payment terms defaults, GST normalization, encrypted bank detail round-trip), reducing confidence for the expanded supplier-management contract.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The scrutiny-validator procedure currently assumes Task-based subagents are always spawnable and does not provide a fallback execution path when mission model configuration breaks Task launches.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: All three Task launches for this milestone failed with 'Invalid model: custom:CLIProxyAPI-5.3-codex-xhigh-pro', preventing automatic reviewer execution.

summary: Fail. Supplier and GRN/return improvements are implemented, but the feature misses its own full PO lifecycle contract because DRAFT/VOID lifecycle realizations are not implemented in runtime API/service transitions.
